accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text or accurate context (COVID-19 reason for the emergency is well-known real-world context).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; proclamation number, Fed. Reg. cite, dates, and statutory reference all match the bill text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the joint resolution terminates the COVID-19 national emergency declared March 13, 2020.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes invocation of Section 202 of the National Emergencies Act as the mechanism, and that the resolution terminates the existing emergency.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill has no material exceptions or conditions.

effective_dates.why: Notes the approval date of April 10, 2023.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the single operative provision: termination of the emergency via Proclamation 9994.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language throughout with no advocacy.

coherent.why: Well-organized, flows logically from context to authority to legislative history.

concise.why: Appropriately detailed for the bill's scope; no padding.

correct_entities.why: National Emergencies Act, Proclamation 9994, and statutory citations are all correct.

correct_figures.why: 85 Fed. Reg. 15337, 50 U.S.C. 1622, and all dates match the bill text.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
